data.title: Stock Today: TNB Trades Flat Midday Amid Volatile Global Energy Prices
data.description:
Tenaga Nasional Bhd traded flat at RM14.14 as of 12.35 pm, with about 1.63 million shares changing hands.

The utility counter opened at RM14.12 and moved between RM14.00 and RM14.18 during the session, with a buy queue at RM14.14 and sellers at RM14.16.

The stock’s performance comes as global energy markets remain volatile following the escalation of the Iran war, which has disrupted supplies through the Strait of Hormuz. Brent crude oil prices have surged to around US$110 per barrel year to date from about US$60 previously, while European natural gas prices have climbed to roughly EUR55 per megawatt hour from EUR30. Australian coal prices have also risen to about US$130 per metric tonne from US$110 amid concerns over supply disruptions in one of the world’s most critical energy corridors.

Despite the sharp rise in energy prices, Tenaga’s earnings exposure to fuel cost fluctuations is partly cushioned by the Automated Fuel Adjustment (AFA) mechanism under Regulatory Period 4 for 2025 to 2027. The monthly adjustment replaced the previous Imbalance Cost Pass-Through system and allows electricity tariffs to reflect fuel cost movements more quickly, helping the utility manage cash flow during periods of volatile energy prices.

Tenaga recently set the AFA rate at minus 2.15 sen per kilowatt hour for March 2026 and indicated that April’s adjustment could be around minus 1.89 sen per kilowatt hour, even as spot prices for coal and gas remain elevated.

Analysts note that the pass-through of fuel costs typically occurs with a lag, as coal shipments and domestic gas pricing are based on earlier average prices, which helps shield Tenaga from immediate cost spikes. The group’s generation arm could also benefit from higher coal fuel margins should coal prices continue rising.

data.title: Nintendo Shares Soar Over 10% As Surprise Hit Pokemon Lift Mood
data.description:
Nintendo Co. shares gained as much as 10.5% in their steepest climb since April as the surprise success of its new Pokémon game helped offset worries around rising memory costs.

The rally comes as physical copies of Pokémon Pokopia, released exclusively for the Switch 2 console on March 5, have sold out at several major retailers in the US.Switch 2 momentum is “surging thanks to the viral hit,” helping to counteract “memory cost headwinds” that have weighed on Nintendo’s stock since late 2025, wrote Jefferies analyst Atul Goyal in a note. Amazon has already raised the price of the Pokémon title to around $80 in a sign of its popularity, he added.

However, even taking into account Wednesday’s climb, Nintendo shares have lost almost 30% from a recent peak in November. The shares were trading around ¥10,000 as of 12:30 p.m. in Tokyo.

Bloomberg

data.title: SGX Opens Lower As Wall Street Caution And Oil Volatility Weigh On Sentiment
data.description:
Singapore’s SGX opened lower with the Straits Times Index (STI) down 7.40 points or 0.15% to 4,853.24 as trading got underway.

The benchmark index tracked cautious sentiment from Wall Street, where lingering uncertainty over the US-Iran conflict and swings in crude oil prices have prompted a mixed outlook.

Market breadth at the open showed 98 advancers against 71 decliners, with total volume reaching 73.40 million shares valued at S$142.53 million.

Major counters including DBS slipped 0.08 points to S$55.57, while OCBC Bank was at S$20.88, UOB at S$36.21, and Singtel at S$5.01. YZJ Shipbuilding traded at S$4.13 and ST Engineering at S$10.94.

Derivative activity remained moderate, with 22,240 contracts traded in equity index futures. The MSCI Singapore Index Futures stood at 439.25, while FTSE China A50 Index Futures were at 14,718.00. Currency futures included USD/SGD at 1.2716 and USD/CNH at 6.8703.

Analysts noted that optimism from US President Donald Trump’s remarks about a potential end to the conflict with Iran has been tempered by volatility in oil markets.

Crude prices surged back above US$90 per barrel after tumbling overnight, while global indices in Asia opened mixed, with the Hang Seng Index surging 2.2% to 25,959.90 and Japan’s Nikkei 225 rising 2.9% amid easing war-related risk.

Investors remain cautious, with the STI likely to mirror Wall Street’s initial pullback amid global uncertainties, while selective buying was seen in resilient blue-chip and financial counters.

data.title: Stock Today: NEXG Climbs 3.6% Amid Board Turmoil And Legal Challenges
data.description:
NEXG Bhd’s stock edged higher by 3.57% to RM0.290 as of 4.07 pm, with 8.7 million shares traded.

The counter opened at RM0.280 and touched an intraday high of RM0.300.

The increase comes amid ongoing corporate upheaval for the technology group, formerly known as Datasonic.

The company recently suspended the executive powers of its Executive Chairman and CEO, Datuk Haji Abu Hanifah Noordin, following a RM145.6 million fair value adjustment on its investment portfolio that resulted in a net loss of RM130.88 million for the third quarter ended December 31, 2025.

In addition, Executive Director Datuk Chong Loong Men resigned on March 8, citing personal reasons, less than four months after joining the board.

Days later, NEXG was served with a writ of summons by two shareholders seeking to restrain the company and certain board members from making key corporate decisions ahead of an extraordinary general meeting scheduled for April 3, 2026.

Further complicating the group’s operations, NEXG Bina confirmed that 16 of its bank accounts have been frozen since mid-November 2025 under an Anti-Money Laundering investigation by the Royal Malaysia Police.

The company said the continued freeze could affect its financial and operational position, forcing it to rely on collections from its manufacturing division to meet fixed costs.

Despite the turbulence, NEXG’s stock has managed to rebound modestly, reflecting cautious investor sentiment as the company navigates legal, operational, and governance challenges.

data.title: SGX Opens Lower As Wall Street Retreats Amid Oil Rebound
data.description:
Singapore stocks opened lower, reflecting cautious investor sentiment after Wall Street ended mixed in a choppy session and global oil prices surged.

The Straits Times Index (STI) slipped 9.41 points, or 0.19%, to 4,854.40 by 9.17 am, as market participants digested mixed cues from Wall Street and ongoing geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Trading volume reached 215.15 million shares, with a total value of S$241.98 million. Advancers numbered 112 while decliners were 137.

Regional investor focus was drawn to the rebound in crude oil prices, which gained nearly 4 percent following a steep drop on Tuesday. The surge came amid reports of additional projectiles striking vessels off Iran’s coast and Iran’s reported plan to mine the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over the free flow of oil from the Middle East.

Among key Singapore-listed counters, DBS fell 0.36% to S$55.52, UOB dipped to S$36.46, and OCBC Bank edged down to S$20.84. Industrial and technology-linked stocks including Keppel and ST Engineering also traded lower in early trade.

Derivatives activity was moderate, with 55,460 contracts traded, including the MSCI Singapore Index Futures at 437.50.

The cautious tone follows Wall Street’s mixed performance on Tuesday, where the Dow fell 34.29 points, or 0.1%, to 47,706.51, the S&P 500 declined 14.51 points, or 0.2%, to 6,781.48, and the Nasdaq gained just 1.16 points to 22,697.10. Analysts noted that the rebound in oil, after an almost 12 percent drop the previous day, weighed on investor sentiment.

In the United States, consumer price data for February came in line with expectations, with headline CPI up 0.3% month-on-month and core CPI up 0.2%. Annual growth rates remained at 2.4% and 2.5% respectively. These figures were closely watched by markets for signals on the Federal Reserve’s interest rate path.

Investors in Asia also monitored corporate earnings, with Oracle posting stronger-than-expected results and lifting its 2027 revenue outlook, providing a partial boost to technology-linked markets.

Crude oil gains were mirrored in Singapore energy-related plays, while broader market sentiment remained tentative ahead of further global developments on the Iran conflict and upcoming U.S. economic data.

data.title: Bursa Opens Lower As Oil Rebound Dampens Sentiment
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened lower, with all major indices retreating amid cautious investor sentiment following mixed signals from Wall Street and a rebound in crude oil prices.

The benchmark FBMKLCI fell 12.06 points, or 0.71%, to 1,696.72 by 9.11 am. Other key indices were also in the red, with the FBM70 down 62.39 points, or 0.36%, to 17,100.05, the F4GBM slipping 5.84 points, or 0.58%, to 1,001.74, the FBMEMAS retreating 73.25 points, or 0.59%, to 12,410.89, and the FBMSHA easing 35.58 points, or 0.29%, to 12,127.96.

Among the most active counters, OGX led trading volume at 326.67 million shares, closing 0.045 lower at RM0.305. AAX fell 0.06 to RM1.14 on 12.05 million shares, while HIBISCS gained 0.06 to RM2.26 with 10.03 million shares traded. HENGYUAN rose 0.10 to RM1.66 on 8.91 million shares, and CAPITALA retreated 0.01 to RM0.415 with 7.89 million shares exchanging hands.

Top gainers in early trade included UTDPLT, up 0.26 to RM31.90, PCHEM rising 0.25 to RM4.39, and MPI gaining 0.22 to RM30.20. Among the laggards, NESTLE slipped 0.90 to RM102.00, PINEAPP fell 0.22 to RM0.60, and CARLSBG eased 0.14 to RM17.32.

The cautious start reflected global market developments, with U.S. futures pointing to a softer open after a mixed Wall Street session. Crude oil for April delivery rebounded nearly 4% following Tuesday’s almost 12% drop, as geopolitical tensions in the Middle East intensified. Reports of projectiles striking vessels off Iran’s coast and fears of Iran mining the Strait of Hormuz added to supply concerns.

U.S. economic data released on Wednesday showed consumer prices for February increased in line with expectations, with headline CPI up 0.3% month-on-month and core CPI rising 0.2%. Annual rates remained unchanged at 2.4% and 2.5% respectively, providing insight into the Federal Reserve’s interest rate trajectory.

Investors also remained alert to regional developments, including oil supply concerns and upcoming U.S. reports on crude inventories, which could influence sentiment on Bursa in the coming sessions.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Flat Against US Dollar, Firmer Against Other Majors
data.description:
The ringgit opened firmer against a basket of major currencies but was flat against the US dollar on Wednesday, supported by resilient domestic economic data amid heightened geopolitical tensions and mixed global signals, as reported by Bernama.

At 8 am, the local currency stood unchanged at 3.9200/9355 against the greenback from Tuesday’s close of 3.9200/9260.

According to the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM), the country’s Industrial Production Index (IPI) rose 5.9 per cent year-on-year in January 2026, driven mainly by stronger performance in the manufacturing sector.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid told Bernama that historical economic data confirm that economic activities have been resilient.

“However, it remains to be seen how such momentum can be carried through in the months following the shock from the war in Iran.

“Some countries are now grappling with high oil prices as fear over inflationary pressures seeps in,” he said, adding that the ringgit is expected to remain sideways against the US dollar today, possibly in the region of RM3.9000 to RM3.9500.

He added that the situation in West Asia continues to hog the limelight.

“Conflicting signals from the Trump administration have led to volatile moves in Brent crude, which touched as high as US$94.8 per barrel but later stabilised around US$87.80 per barrel.

“The US Dollar Index (DXY) fell 0.35 per cent to 98.826 points while spot gold prices were hovering at US$5,192.74 per ounce,” he said.

He said economic data showed that China’s exports jumped 39.6 per cent in February after growing at a rate of 10 per cent in the previous month, and export growth to the US turned positive at 9.7 per cent after remaining in contraction since April last year.

Similarly, Japan’s fourth-quarter 2025 gross domestic product came in better than expected at 1.3 per cent after declining by 2.3 per cent in the preceding quarter.

At the open, the ringgit rose against the Japanese yen to 2.4796/4896 from 2.4859/4899 at Tuesday’s close, climbed against the British pound to 5.2606/2814 from 5.2744/2824 and was firmer against the euro at 4.5519/5699 from 4.5668/5738.

The local note traded mixed against most ASEAN currencies.

It edged up against the Singapore dollar to 3.0784/0910 from 3.0806/0855, and fell against the Thai baht to 12.4055/4659 from 12.3941/4205.

It was flat against the Indonesian rupiah at 232.4/233.4 from 232.4/232.9 and remained unchanged against the Philippine peso at 6.65/6.68 from 6.65/6.67 on Tuesday.

data.title: Chinese Stocks Open Marginally Lower
data.description:
Chinese stocks opened lower on Thursday, with the benchmark Shanghai Composite Index down 0.01 percent to open at 4,133.20 points.

The Shenzhen Component Index opened 0.05 percent lower at 14,457.63 points

data.title: Bursa Extend Gains On Midday As Oil Votality Clouds Market
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended gains at midday, with the FBM KLCI rising 2.67 points or 0.16 per cent to 1,704.35, while the broader FBM70 led sector indices with a 101.96-point or 0.60 per cent gain to 17,166.66.

The F4GBM index was up 2.43 points or 0.24 per cent at 1,005.65, FBMEMAS climbed 30.76 points or 0.25 per cent to 12,458.56, and FBMSHA added 25.80 points or 0.21 per cent to 12,127.45.

Market activity saw ZETRIX, AAX, and GDB among the most actively traded counters, while PETDAG, F&N, and CARLSBG topped the gainers. NESTLE, MPI, and PETGAS led declines.

Investors continued to track developments in the Middle East, where the US and Israel’s strikes on Iran, and Tehran’s retaliatory attacks, have disrupted oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

Brent and West Texas Intermediate crude surged close to US$120 a barrel on Monday before easing about five per cent on Wednesday after reports suggested the International Energy Agency is considering a record release from reserves to counter soaring prices.

Asian equities were broadly higher, with Tokyo and Seoul posting strong gains, while Shanghai and Singapore edged lower. Analysts note that the Strait of Hormuz remains the critical focal point for energy markets, with any disruption potentially reigniting supply fears and sending crude prices sharply higher.

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Opens Slightly Mixed As Global Oil Volatility Weighs
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened mixed, with the FBM KLCI marginally lower at 1,701.63, down 0.05 points.

Other key indices saw moderate gains and losses, with the FBM70 rising 16.35 points (+0.10%) to 17,081.05, the F4GBM up 0.16 points (+0.02%) at 1,003.38 and the FBMEMAS adding 2.57 points (+0.02%) to 12,430.37. Meanwhile, the FBMSHA fell 14.03 points (-0.12%) to 12,087.62.

Among individual counters, PETDAG led the gainers at RM21.72 (+0.22), followed by KESM at RM2.96 (+0.17) and MISC at RM8.48 (+0.16). NESTLE led the decliners at RM102.50 (-1.50), while PETGAS eased 0.16 to RM17.44 and CEPCO dropped 0.12 to RM1.10.

Market activity remained focused on mid- and small-cap stocks, with PHB, TWL and AAX among the most actively traded by volume.

According to RTTNews, analysts cited lingering concerns over the US-Iran conflict and oil price volatility as key factors weighing on early trading sentiment.

Crude oil has rebounded above US$90 a barrel after a near 11% overnight plunge, following President Donald Trump’s remarks suggesting a “very complete” state of the conflict in the Middle East, while European and US markets remain cautious.

data.title: Stock Today: PetChem Rose 2.29% Amid Gains In Global Petrochemical Prices
data.description:
Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd (PetChem) rose 2.29% to RM4.02, with 9.64 million shares traded as of 3.16 pm, amid strong gains in global petrochemical prices following geopolitical tensions in the Middle East.

CGS International noted PetChemsold urea at around US$650 per tonne, up from US$490-510 per tonne before the US-Israel-Iran conflict, while metanol prices increased to roughly US$400 per tonne from US$320. The surge in prices is attributed to supply disruptions of key raw materials such as naphtha, LPG, and methane from the region, as well as temporary shutdowns of petrochemical plants.

CGSI expects the higher selling prices across PetChem’s Fertilisers & Methanol and Olefins & Derivatives segments could exceed previous forecasts, benefiting low-cost producers like PCG.

Analysts maintain an “Add” rating with a target price of RM4.45, citing the potential for further gains if disruptions at the Strait of Hormuz continue.

PetChem opened at RM3.82, hitting a high of RM4.06 and a low of RM3.77 during the session.

data.title: FBM KLCI Gains On Oil Supply Hopes Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
The FBM KLCI closed higher as improving market sentiment, boosted by reports of a potential record oil stock release by the International Energy Agency (IEA), lifted investors’ risk appetite.

CIMB Securities noted that the benchmark index settled 7.1 points, or 0.42%, higher at 1,708.78, after touching an intraday high of 1,709.93, with banking shares contributing to the gains.

There were 605 gainers, 447 losers and 525 counters traded unchanged on Bursa Malaysia. Turnover stood at 2.71 billion shares valued at RM2.8 billion.

Reports that the IEA is considering the largest-ever coordinated release of strategic oil reserves eased concerns over supply disruptions amid the U.S.-Israel war involving Iran. Stephen Innes, managing partner at SPI Asset Management, said Asian equities and global futures steadied as crude prices held just below the psychologically important US$90 per barrel level. “The US$90 mark has become a key pressure point for markets this week, as prices below that level tend to support risk sentiment, while a break above it could quickly tighten financial conditions,” he noted.

Among gainers on Bursa Malaysia, Carlsberg rose 48 sen to RM17.46, PETRONAS Dagangan added 40 sen to RM21.90, United Plantations gained 38 sen to RM31.64 and Chin Teck Plantations climbed 26 sen to RM10.80. In contrast, Nestle fell RM1.10 to RM102.90, Malaysian Pacific Industries dropped 56 sen to RM29.98, QL Resources lost 13 sen to RM3.89 and Pentamaster declined 12 sen to RM3.08.

Foreign funds and retail investors were net sellers, offloading equities worth RM101 million and RM5 million respectively, while local institutions were net buyers at RM105 million.

The ringgit strengthened 0.23% against the US dollar to 3.9150 and 0.33% against the Singapore dollar to 3.0732.

Regional bourses mostly advanced, with Japan’s Nikkei 225 up 1.43% and South Korea’s Kospi rising 1.4%, while Singapore’s Straits Times Index and Hong Kong’s Hang Seng slipped slightly.

data.title: Carlsberg Leads Bursa Gainers While Nestlé Tops Decliners
data.description:
Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Berhad emerged as the top gainer on Bursa Malaysia today, climbing RM0.48 to close at RM17.46 on a volume of 63,900 shares.

Petronas Dagangan Berhad followed, rising RM0.40 to RM21.90 with 1,413,800 shares traded, while United Plantations Berhad gained RM0.38 to RM31.64 on volume of 225,800 shares. Chin Teck Plantations Berhad added RM0.26 to finish at RM10.80, and Allianz Malaysia Berhad rose RM0.24 to RM21.54 with 103,200 shares changing hands.

On the downside, Nestlé (Malaysia) Berhad led the decliners, slipping RM1.10 to RM102.90 with 291,700 shares traded. Malaysian Pacific Industries Berhad fell RM0.56 to RM29.98 on volume of 81,500 shares, while QL Resources Berhad declined RM0.13 to RM3.89 with 2,034,500 shares changing hands.

Meanwhile, CEPCO Berhad dropped RM0.12 to RM1.10 on volume of 201,900 shares, and Penta Technology Solutions Holdings Berhad slipped RM0.12 to RM3.08 with 584,800 shares traded.

data.title: Hang Seng Futures Slip Below 26,000 As Weakness Persists
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) maintained its negative trading bias on Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) after the contract failed to extend its recent rebound and slipped below the key 26,000-point psychological level.

The futures contract fell 91 points on Wednesday to settle at 25,820 points. It opened slightly weaker at 25,909 points before sliding sharply to an intraday low of 25,706 points near the close.

In evening trading, the contract pared some losses, rebounding 64 points to last trade at 25,756 points.

RHB Research said the retreat below the 26,000-point level following the recent rebound suggests the market may be entering a period of temporary price weakness.

“The pullback below the 26,000-pt psychological level following the recent rebound suggests a temporary price weakness,” the research house said, adding that it expects the contract to move in a rangebound pattern in the near term.

Technical indicators also point to a cautious outlook. The Relative Strength Index remains below the 50% mark, signalling that downside pressure could persist.

Based on the latest correction, RHB Research advised traders to maintain the short position initiated at the close on Feb 26 at 26,367 points.

To manage trading risk, the stop-loss level is set at 27,500 points.

On the technical chart, immediate support is seen at 24,500 points, followed by 23,500 points. Meanwhile, resistance levels are identified at 26,600 points and 27,500 points.

data.title: Ringgit Extends Gains At Opening As Oil Prices Stabilise
data.description:
The ringgit extended gains against the US dollar and other currencies at Thursday’s opening, supported by stabilising oil prices following the International Energy Agency’s (IEA) decision to release additional supply into the market, an economist said to Bernama.

At 8 am, the local currency edged up to 3.9130/9350 against the greenback from Wednesday’s close of 3.9175/9230.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said oil prices remained a key focus for the market after the IEA’s 32 member countries agreed to release 400 million barrels from strategic petroleum reserves.

The United States also followed the move, announcing the release of 172 million barrels of oil into the domestic market.

“Such decisions aim to stabilise oil prices, especially at the retail level.

“Clearly, the Iran war has taken a toll on retail oil prices, as rising costs can undermine consumers’ purchasing power,” he told Bernama.

At the time of writing, Brent crude rose 7.55 per cent to US$98.92 per barrel, while New York Mercantile Exchange (NYMEX) natural gas prices increased 1.25 per cent to US$3.25 per million British Thermal Units.

“Leaders across the globe are preoccupied with global price pressures and are taking steps to ensure retail fuel prices remain under control,” he said.

Meanwhile, the US Federal Reserve appears set to maintain its restrictive monetary policy stance when the Federal Open Market Committee meets next week.

Amid heightened uncertainties, Mohd Afzanizam expects the ringgit to trade between RM3.9000 and RM3.9500 against the US dollar today.

At the opening, the ringgit rose against the Japanese yen to 2.4596/4738 from 2.4719/4755 at Wednesday’s close, strengthened against the British pound to 5.2360/2654 from 5.2545/2619, and was firmer against the euro at 4.5164/5418 from 4.5412/5475.

The local currency also traded higher against most ASEAN currencies.

It edged up against the Singapore dollar to 3.0661/0839 from 3.0754/0800, and gained against the Thai baht to 12.2346/3119 from 12.3386/3637.

The ringgit strengthened against the Indonesian rupiah to 231.7/233.1 from 232.0/232.4 and improved against the Philippine peso to 6.61/6.66 from 6.62/6.64 on Wednesday.

data.title: RHB Keeps Bearish Bias On Hang Seng Index Futures Despite Rebound
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has maintained its short trading stance on the Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) despite the contract extending its rebound during Tuesday’s session.

The research house said HSIF climbed 644 points to close at 25,911, up from 25,295 at the open. The index touched an intraday high of 25,931 points before ending the session near that level, while evening trading saw it edge up a further 25 points to 25,936 points.

RHB Research noted that the latest price movement indicates the index is approaching the 20-day simple moving average, signalling that bullish momentum is strengthening.

However, the contract is still trading below both the 20-day and 50-day moving averages, suggesting the broader technical outlook remains weak.

The research house expects strong resistance around the 26,600 level and said bears should retain the technical advantage as long as the index remains below 27,500.

Despite the recent “higher high” rebound, RHB Research said it is maintaining its bearish trading bias and advised traders to keep the short position initiated at the close of Feb 26 at 26,367 points.

To manage risk, the stop-loss level has been set at 27,500 points.

On the downside, support is seen at 24,500, followed by 23,500, while resistance is at 26,600 and 27,500.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates March 11, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on March 11, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Stock Picks: Westports And Guan Chong
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Westports Holdings Bhd and Guan Chong Bhd as stocks with near-term bullish momentum following technical breakouts.

The research house said Westports Holdings could chart a stronger upward trajectory after breaking above resistance with a bullish candlestick formation. The rising 21 day simple moving average also suggests the counter may sustain its positive momentum in the short term.

If the stock manages to hold above the RM6 breakout level, RHB Research said continued buying interest could push the price towards RM6.25 before testing the RM6.45 level.

However, the bullish outlook would be invalidated if the counter falls below the RM5.80 support level, which may trigger a correction.

Meanwhile, Guan Chong is expected to extend its upside movement after breaking past resistance on strong trading volume.

RHB Research noted the counter printed a long white candlestick and closed above the RM0.75 resistance level, signalling strong bullish momentum.

Premised on the renewed strength, the research house said the stock is now targeting the RM0.79 level followed by RM0.83.

A decline below the RM0.71 support level would negate the bullish setup and potentially trigger a correction.

data.title: Wall Street Slips As Iran War Escalation Shakes Investor Sentiment
data.description:
US stocks closed mostly lower on Wednesday as investors turned their attention away from benign inflation data and focused instead on rising geopolitical tensions linked to the US-Israeli war with Iran.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average posted the steepest decline among the major indexes, falling 289.24 points or 0.61% to 47,417.27. The S&P 500 slipped 5.68 points or 0.08% to 6,775.80, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite edged up 19.03 points or 0.08% to 22,716.14.

Trading remained volatile throughout the session as investors weighed conflicting signals around oil supply. Iran continued attacking vessels in the blockaded Strait of Hormuz, heightening concerns over energy disruptions. At the same time, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries said Saudi Arabia had increased production while the International Energy Agency agreed to release 400 million barrels from strategic reserves in a bid to stabilise markets.

Oil prices surged on the developments, with front-month West Texas Intermediate crude rising 4.6% and Brent crude climbing 4.8% at settlement.

Inflation data released earlier in the day showed US consumer prices rising broadly in line with expectations. The US Department of Labor reported that February’s Consumer Price Index indicated inflation remained moderate, leaving annual price growth within half a percentage point of the US Federal Reserve target of 2%.

However, markets largely brushed aside the report because it predated the outbreak of the conflict that has since pushed crude prices sharply higher.

Inflation concerns intensified after Iran’s military command warned that oil could potentially reach US$200 per barrel if tensions continue.

“In such an uncertain environment, the markets and investors are kind of starving for any signal, in one direction or another,” said Matthew Keator, managing partner at wealth management firm Keator Group.

“There have been these false or inaccurate reports, and the markets are swinging on that type of news.”

Keator added that rising energy prices could directly affect consumer spending.

“It’s all about the consumer, and how the shock of a sustained increase in oil prices is going to affect the consumer’s pocketbook and their spending habits,” he said.

Among the 11 major sectors in the S&P 500, consumer staples posted the largest decline while energy stocks outperformed, climbing 2.5% as crude prices surged.

Technology shares were also marginally higher, supported by strong gains in Oracle Corporation, which jumped 9.2% after issuing upbeat revenue guidance tied to continued growth in artificial intelligence spending through 2027.

Meanwhile, financial stocks faced pressure following reports that JPMorgan Chase had marked down certain loans linked to private-credit groups and tightened lending to the sector. Shares of Ares Management fell 4.8% while Apollo Global Management dropped 1.9%.

Elsewhere, Campbell Soup Company slid 7.1% after cutting its annual forecasts and warning of increasing pressure from revised US tariffs later this year. Defence contractor AeroVironment also declined 6.3% after forecasting 2026 adjusted profit below market estimates.

Looking ahead, investors are widely expecting the Federal Reserve to keep interest rates unchanged at its next policy meeting while policymakers assess the risks of rising energy prices alongside signs of a cooling labour market.

“I think the word ‘transitory’ may come back,” said Chuck Carlson, chief executive officer at Horizon Investment Services.

“I think they’re probably more concerned about jobs than they are about inflation right now, the spike in oil notwithstanding.”

Reuters

data.title: Chinese Yuan Edges Over Dollar On Strong Export Data
data.description:
The central parity rate of the Chinese currency renminbi, or the yuan, strengthened 65 pips to 6.8917 against the U.S. dollar Wednesday, according to the China Foreign Exchange Trade System.

In China’s spot foreign exchange market, the yuan is allowed to rise or fall by 2 percent from the central parity rate each trading day.

The central parity rate of the yuan against the U.S. dollar is based on a weighted average of prices offered by market makers before the opening of the interbank market each business day.
